metric_value: Aberdeen assesses Sagister as 'must-have' product for running production UNIX applications in enterprise datacenters
notes: Aberdeen's lead endorsement

metric_value: Datacenter must provide new-generation client-server applications for organizational efficiency
notes: Factor 1 of the datacenter paradox Aberdeen identifies

metric_value: Datacenter must maintain mainframe disciplines: 7x24x365 availability, backup, security, reliability, low costs
notes: Factor 2 of the datacenter paradox; UNIX systems in 1996 typically lacked these

metric_value: Bull has over 15 years of experience implementing UNIX systems in customer operations
notes: Bull's credibility claim for Sagister

metric_value: Approximately 200 individuals assigned to the Sagister project
notes: Signals Bull's commitment level to the product

metric_value: Single 6-8 processor node; supports up to 2,000 PC clients; suited for prototyping and development
notes: Entry tier of three Sagister configurations

metric_value: Two-node system; 12-16 processors; supports up to 3,000 PC clients; Aberdeen's recommended production system
notes: Middle tier; Aberdeen's recommendation

metric_value: Four-node FDDI-interconnected; up to 32 PowerPC processors; supports up to 5,000 users; very large databases
notes: Top tier for very large enterprise deployments

metric_name: ROI claim for Sagister Operations Base
metric_value: Operations Base lowers datacenter support costs with less than 7% systems overhead increase; greater than 50% ROI vs alternative UNIX with add-ons
notes: Aberdeen agrees with this claim; vendor-originated figures

metric_value: First major Sagister application Function Set; adds security, automated ops, HA, backup, event management to SAP R/3
notes: SAP R/3 general availability planned Q3 1996

metric_value: AIX 4.1 allows 10,000+ packaged applications to run on Sagister
notes: Key selling point of AIX as the OS foundation

metric_value: RS/6000 SP lacks reliability, manageability, load balancing, availability, backup/restore, ease-of-administration, or security features of Sagister
notes: Aberdeen's analysis; Aberdeen notes RS6000 SP designed for HPC not commercial datacenter

metric_value: HP MC/ServiceGuard may be better at clustering but HP has not undertaken ambitious mainframe-class operational functionality; relies on partners via OpenView
notes: HP philosophy: open framework, partner ecosystem; vs Bull's integrated approach

metric_value: Digital TruCluster with Oracle 7.3 may provide highest throughput of any UNIX platform including Sagister; but Digital has not developed datacenter operational capabilities
notes: DEC conceded highest throughput; Aberdeen notes throughput ≠ complete datacenter solution

metric_value: CA-Unicenter provides similar operations functionality but lacks event management, HA functions, common graphical command icons, and Function Sets
notes: Aberdeen recommends adding CA-Unicenter to any alternative UNIX system comparison

metric_value: Sagister's design philosophy is on target; future MVS interoperability will make it viable for non-Bull enterprises
notes: Aberdeen predicts Sagister can win non-Bull customers with MVS interoperability

metric_value: Bull acquired by Atos SE in October 2014 for €620M; Sagister product discontinued well before acquisition as UNIX midrange market consolidated around IBM, HP, Sun
notes: Source: Bloomberg/Atos 2014; Bull never expanded Sagister to non-Bull customer base as predicted

metric_value: Packaging proven best-of-class technology components (not inventing proprietary tech) with deep mainframe-discipline integration; positioning as 'UNIX Multiframe'
notes: Open systems + value-added integration strategy; contrasts with IBM's proprietary SP approach

metric_value: 1995 revenues FF 26,656M; net profit FF 306M — first profit since 1988
notes: Signals financial recovery and credibility for enterprise buyers

metric_value: Bull intends to implement PowerPC 620 processor in Sagister by fall 1996; OS support dependent on IBM/Bull AIX 64-bit efforts
notes: Roadmap item; 64-bit Oracle VLM support planned 1997

metric_value: IS professionals consistently state UNIX is a step down from GCOS/MVS/VMS in manageability, reliability, availability, security; Sagister fills this gap
notes: Aberdeen's core thesis for Sagister's value proposition

metric_value: The broader need for mainframe-discipline UNIX management proved correct; met by ITIL frameworks, HP OpenView, IBM Tivoli, and eventually cloud-native ops practices
notes: The thesis was correct; Sagister specifically did not win the market

metric_value: CLAM HACMP supports 8-node clusters; planned 16 shortly, 32 by end of 1997
notes: HACMP (now IBM PowerHA) confirmed this trajectory

metric_value: Two-fold challenge: (1) convincing experienced UNIX users of operational cost savings; (2) convincing mainframe users that UNIX operational problems are real
notes: Aberdeen accurately identifies the go-to-market barriers Bull faced

metric_value: Bull will support up to 16 CPUs per system by end of 1997 (from current 8 CPUs max in SMP)
notes: Capacity scaling roadmap for enterprise growth
